introduction: Welcome to the Electroanalytical Virtual Laboratory (EVL) — an innovative, research-oriented digital platform designed for Undergraduate (UG) students, Postgraduate (PG) scholars, research fellows, and faculty members. EVL aims to introduce students/learners with the fundamentals of redox chemistry and the concepts of electrochemistry through a well-designed virtual experiment of voltammetry, amperometry, potentiometry etc in a lucid manner. It provides better understanding of the relationships between electrical charges, potential, current, 3 electrode system etc. Electroanalysis plays a vital role in modern chemical sciences, environmental monitoring, material science, and sensor development. This serves as a comprehensive academic resource to strengthen knowledge, enhance analytical skills, and promote research innovation in the field of electroanalytical chemistry. This laboratory introduces learners to the core principles of oxidation-reduction chemistry, electrode processes, and electrochemical measurements through structured modules and simulation-based experiments.

experiments.name: Electrolysis: Faraday Constant and Avogadro Number
Potentiometric Titration of a Strong Acid and Strong Base
Heavy Metal Analysis using Differential Pulse Voltammetry (DPV) and Square Wave Voltammetry (SWV) Techniques
Redox Analyte Study on Different Electrodes
Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy Study of Ferricyanide System using Different Working Electrodes

UG: B.Sc. students, UG learners, research scholars, and faculty members, with a strong emphasis on core and applied electrochemical concepts.
PG: M.Sc. students, PG learners, research scholars, and faculty members, with a strong emphasis on core and applied electrochemical concepts.

objective:
• To develop a clear understanding of redox chemistry, electrode processes, and electrochemical thermodynamics. To build strong conceptual foundations in voltammetry, amperometry, and potentiometry.
  • To simulate real-time electroanalytical experiments in a safe and accessible digital environment. To enhance analytical reasoning and problem-solving skills through guided virtual experiments.
  • To provide in-depth understanding of surface modification techniques for electrode enhancement. To explore nanomaterials and composite materials for improved sensor performance.
  • To develop methodologies for electrochemical detection of toxic heavy metals in real environmental samples. To promote practical knowledge in trace-level analysis of contaminants such as lead, cadmium, and mercury. To train learners in sample preparation, calibration techniques, and validation methods.
  • To foster innovation in electrochemical sensor development and environmental applications. To prepare learners for advanced academic research and industrial analytical practices.

courseAlignment.description: The syllabi of this lab aligns to the following universities in India.
universities: This lab aligns with courses and syllabi of chemical sciences, chemical engineering with electrochemistry-based topics such as cells, potentiometry, polarography, amperometry etc. It supports University and AICTE-prescribed curricula in a broader spectrum.
